data.content:
Here's the full diagnosis:

---

## Build Failure Analysis — `BuildDiag.sln`

### Solution structure (3 projects)
```
Core.csproj          ✅ Built successfully
Services.csproj      ❌ Failed (ROOT CAUSE)
Api.csproj           ⚠️  Skipped — cascading failure
```

### Root Cause — `Services.csproj`

**Missing NuGet package: `Newtonsoft.Json`**

`Services/UserService.cs` uses `Newtonsoft.Json` (lines 2–3: `using Newtonsoft.Json;` / `using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;`, line 22: `JObject`), but `Services.csproj` has **no `PackageReference`** for it. Three CS0246 errors result:

| Line | Error | Symbol |
|------|-------|--------|
| 2 | CS0246 | `Newtonsoft` (namespace) |
| 3 | CS0246 | `Newtonsoft` (namespace) |
| 22 | CS0246 | `JObject` (type) |

### Cascading Failure — `Api.csproj`

`Api.csproj` has a `ProjectReference` to `Services.csproj`. Because Services failed, MSBuild logged:
> *"Target 'Build' skipped. Previously built unsuccessfully."*

Api was never compiled — it has **no errors of its own**.

### Fix

Add the missing package reference to `Services/Services.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
</ItemGroup>
```

Or run:
```
dotnet add Services/Services.csproj package Newtonsoft.Json
```

This single fix resolves all 3 errors and unblocks the Api project build.
